evidence.scientific_clarity: Grouped bar chart clearly shows 4 methods × 5 benchmarks with error bars; OursModel winner highlighted via bold outline and bold legend text; y-range 30–80 avoids wasted space.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' correctly set; font.family='sans-serif' declared but no explicit font override, so matplotlib falls back to DejaVu Sans (the default the brief warns against); font sizes 7–8pt are reasonable for single-column.
evidence.palette: Wong colorblind-safe palette (#999999, #56B4E9, #E69F00, #009E73) used correctly; hatches added for B&W readers; green reserved for the winning method (directional use is defensible here, not a red/green confusion issue).
evidence.layout_density: figsize 3.5×2.6 in (single-column ready); tight_layout(pad=0.4) applied; y-range computed dynamically from data ±4pp; no visible label overlap; horizontal grid aids reading.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and names the winner; legend placed upper-left inside axes; error bar cap markers inherit matplotlib default blue (#1f77b4) rather than the specified ecolor (#333333), which a careful reviewer would flag.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is valid vector (21 KB, 18 streams, TrueType fonts embedded with pdf.fonttype=42); pdffonts confirms CID TrueType with Identity-H encoding; text is selectable.
evidence.file_format: All five required artifacts present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (21 KB), figure.png (63 KB), figure.svg (27 KB), caption.tex (415 B), gen_figure.py (2.9 KB); data.json not in cwd but script ran successfully.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states the specific finding (OursModel wins every benchmark), quantifies the margin (3.3–5.8 pp over next-best Method-B), explains error bars (sample std over 5 seeds), and notes grayscale accessibility via hatching.

overall_strengths: Correct colorblind-safe Wong palette with hatches, proper fonttype=42/svg.fonttype=none, and dynamic y-range make this figure technically solid and publication-ready for Nature/CVPR single-column.
Caption is specific and quantitative (3.3–5.8 pp gain named explicitly), matching the figure content precisely and satisfying the brief's requirement to highlight the winning method.
overall_weaknesses: DejaVu Sans (matplotlib default) is used despite font.family='sans-serif' — no explicit font override (e.g., Liberation Sans, Helvetica, or Arial) was provided, violating the brief's 'no DejaVu Sans defaults' requirement.

Error bar cap markers inherit matplotlib's default color cycle (#1f77b4, a blue not in the palette) rather than the intended #333333, creating a minor visual inconsistency that a detail-oriented reviewer would flag.
